title: InstCombine: Order shufflevector operands by complexity

tests.source_program:
declare <4 x i32> @get_vector()
define <4 x i32> @shuffle_call_vs_undef() {
entry:
  %call = call <4 x i32> @get_vector()
  %shuf = shufflevector <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> %call, <4 x i32> <i32 4, i32 5, i32 6, i32 7>
  ret <4 x i32> %shuf
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on undef/poison but doesn't mention function calls as complexity sources. This tests ordering when one operand is a call instruction.

}

tests.source_program:
define <4 x i32> @shuffle_load_vs_constant_complexity(<4 x i32>* %ptr) {
entry:
  %load = load <4 x i32>, <4 x i32>* %ptr
  %const = shufflevector <4 x i32> zeroinitializer, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0>
  %shuf = shufflevector <4 x i32> %const, <4 x i32> %load, <4 x i32> <i32 4, i32 5, i32 1, i32 2>
  ret <4 x i32> %shuf
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define <4 x i32> @shuffle_load_vs_constant_complexity(<4 x i32>* %ptr) {
entry:
  %load = load <4 x i32>, <4 x i32>* %ptr
  %const = shufflevector <4 x i32> zeroinitializer, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0>
  %shuf = shufflevector <4 x i32> %load, <4 x i32> %const, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 1, i32 4, i32 4>
  ret <4 x i32> %shuf
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Tests load instruction complexity vs constant - summary focuses on undef/poison/constants but not memory operations as complexity factors

alive2.src:

}

  %shuf = shufflevector <4 x i32> %const, <4 x i32> %load, 4, 5, 1, 2

}

tests.source_program:
define <4 x float> @shuffle_nested_shuffle_operands(<4 x float> %a, <4 x float> %b) {
entry:
  %inner = shufflevector <4 x float> %a, <4 x float> %b, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 1, i32 4, i32 5>
  %simple = bitcast <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0> to <4 x float>
  %outer = shufflevector <4 x float> %simple, <4 x float> %inner, <4 x i32> <i32 4, i32 5, i32 2, i32 3>
  ret <4 x float> %outer
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define <4 x float> @shuffle_nested_shuffle_operands(<4 x float> %a, <4 x float> %b) {
entry:
  %inner = shufflevector <4 x float> %a, <4 x float> %b, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 1, i32 4, i32 5>
  %simple = bitcast <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0> to <4 x float>
  %outer = shufflevector <4 x float> %inner, <4 x float> %simple, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 1, i32 6, i32 7>
  ret <4 x float> %outer
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary covers insert/extract and bitcasts but does not explicitly address nested shufflevector instructions as operands. The inner shuffle has higher complexity than the bitcast constant, triggering reordering.

alive2.src:
define <4 x float> @shuffle_nested_shuffle_operands(<4 x float> %a, <4 x float> %b) {
entry:
  %inner = shufflevector <4 x float> %a, <4 x float> %b, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 1, i32 4, i32 5>
  %simple = bitcast <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0> to <4 x float>
  %outer = shufflevector <4 x float> %simple, <4 x float> %inner, <4 x i32> <i32 4, i32 5, i32 2, i32 3>
  ret <4 x float> %outer
}

; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define <4 x float> @shuffle_nested_shuffle_operands(<4 x float> %a, <4 x float> %b) {
entry:
  %outer = shufflevector <4 x float> %a, <4 x float> <float poison, float poison, float 0.000000e+00, float 0.000000e+00>, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 1, i32 6, i32 7>
  ret <4 x float> %outer
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define <4 x float> @shuffle_nested_shuffle_operands(<4 x float> %a, <4 x float> %b) {
entry:
  %inner = shufflevector <4 x float> %a, <4 x float> %b, 0, 1, 4, 5
  %simple = bitcast <4 x i32> { 0, 0, 0, 0 } to <4 x float>
  %outer = shufflevector <4 x float> %simple, <4 x float> %inner, 4, 5, 2, 3
  ret <4 x float> %outer
}
=>
define <4 x float> @shuffle_nested_shuffle_operands(<4 x float> %a, <4 x float> %b) {
entry:
  %outer = shufflevector <4 x float> %a, <4 x float> { poison, poison, 0.000000, 0.000000 }, 0, 1, 6, 7
  ret <4 x float> %outer
}
Transformation seems to be correct!
